title: Queue reconciliation authority is receipt-bounded

memory.observed: The canonical queue is older than additive queue-update records, and CM-1016 established that a projection requires an explicit base and inclusive terminal commit horizon.
memory.inferred: A terminal commit alone is insufficient: a projection can still omit an eligible update path, substitute altered bytes, or silently include later evidence unless the exact manifest and output bytes are bound by a receipt.
memory.proposed: Treat a reconciled projection as authoritative only within a receipt that binds the canonical queue blob, terminal commit, observed head, exact update manifest, excluded later commits, and projection digest.
memory.superseded: A projection is reproducible merely because it names a terminal commit.
memory.unresolved: No authenticated exhaustive projection and receipt have yet been generated against a full repository checkout.

contradiction_resolution.apparent_conflict: A generated projection may appear newer and more complete than ACTIVE_QUEUE.json while still having an unknown or selective update set.
contradiction_resolution.resolution: Newness is not authority. The canonical file remains the base authority; additive records gain bounded effective authority only when exhaustive inclusion and byte identity are receipt-verifiable.
contradiction_resolution.selection_rule: Use a projection for queue selection only when its receipt validates structurally and runtime checks prove reachability, exhaustive path enumeration within the declared horizon, exact later-commit exclusion, and deterministic output recomputation.

falsification_route: Generate a receipt from a full authenticated checkout, deliberately omit one eligible queue-update path or alter one byte, and require runtime verification to reject the projection.
